1kw-solar-system.html.title: 1kW Solar System Price, Subsidy & Savings Guide (2026)
1kw-solar-system.html.description: Learn the cost, subsidy, savings, annual generation, roof area and panel requirements for a 1kW rooftop solar system in India.

faq.question: How much electricity can a 1kW solar system generate?
faq.answer: A well-designed 1kW solar system typically generates approximately 1,400–1,600 units annually depending on location and weather conditions.

faq.question: How much roof space is required?
faq.answer: Most installations require approximately 80–120 square feet of shadow-free roof area.

faq.question: Can batteries be added later?
faq.answer: Many systems can be upgraded later with battery storage depending on inverter compatibility.

faq.question: Is subsidy guaranteed?
faq.answer: No. Subsidies are subject to government policies, eligibility criteria and approval processes.

learn.html.title: Solar Knowledge Center | Solar AI Advisor
learn.html.description: Free guides and resources to help Indian homeowners understand solar systems, subsidies, net metering, installers, and savings — from India's independent solar advisor.

faq.html.title: Solar FAQ — Common Questions About Rooftop Solar in India | Solar AI Advisor
faq.html.description: Answers to common questions about rooftop solar in India — subsidies, costs, savings, net metering, installer selection, and how Solar AI Advisor works.

faq.question: How does rooftop solar work?
faq.answer: Solar panels convert sunlight into DC electricity. An inverter converts this to AC power for your home. Surplus generation is exported to the grid via net metering, earning credits on future bills.

faq.question: Do solar panels work during cloudy weather?
faq.answer: Yes. Panels generate electricity from diffuse light, not only direct sunlight. Output is reduced on cloudy days but does not stop.

faq.question: Do I need batteries with rooftop solar?
faq.answer: Most on-grid residential systems in India operate without batteries. The grid connection and net metering serve as effective storage for most homeowners.

faq.question: How long does it take to recover the solar investment?
faq.answer: Typical payback is 4–7 years for well-sized on-grid systems in India, after which the system continues generating for 20+ more years.

faq.question: Is the PM Surya Ghar subsidy guaranteed?
faq.answer: No. Subsidies are subject to government policy, fund availability, and eligibility criteria including use of empanelled installers and ALMM-listed panels.

faq.question: What is MNRE empanelment?
faq.answer: MNRE empanelment certifies solar installers to carry out installations under government schemes. It is mandatory for subsidy eligibility but is not a guarantee of quality — always verify installer experience independently.

solar-glossary.html.title: Solar Glossary — Key Solar Energy Terms Explained | Solar AI Advisor
solar-glossary.html.description: Plain-language definitions of 25 solar energy terms used in India — from ALMM and DISCOM to net metering, TOPCon, kWp, CUF, and PM Surya Ghar.

rooftop-solar-prayagraj.html.title: Rooftop Solar in Prayagraj — Cost, Subsidy & PUVVNL Net Metering Guide (2026)
rooftop-solar-prayagraj.html.description: Complete guide to rooftop solar for Prayagraj homeowners — PUVVNL net metering process, PM Surya Ghar subsidy, system costs, and how to choose an empanelled installer in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.

faq.question: Which electricity company handles rooftop solar in Prayagraj?
faq.answer: Prayagraj falls under PUVVNL (Purvanchal Vidyut Vitran Nigam Limited), a subsidiary of UPPCL covering eastern Uttar Pradesh. All net metering applications, bi-directional meter installations, and solar scheme empanelment for Prayagraj homeowners are processed through PUVVNL.

faq.question: How do I apply for net metering with PUVVNL in Prayagraj?
faq.answer: After your rooftop solar system is installed by a PUVVNL-empanelled installer, your installer submits a net metering application to PUVVNL on your behalf. PUVVNL inspects the system and replaces your existing meter with a bi-directional net meter. The process typically takes two to six weeks in Prayagraj.

faq.question: Is PM Surya Ghar subsidy available for Prayagraj homeowners?
faq.answer: Yes. Prayagraj homeowners with a PUVVNL electricity connection are eligible for the PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana central subsidy. You must use a PUVVNL-empanelled installer and apply through pmsuryaghar.gov.in. Subsidy amounts are subject to government policy and fund availability.

faq.question: What solar system size suits most Prayagraj homes?
faq.answer: For typical Prayagraj residential homes with monthly PUVVNL bills of ₹1,500–₹4,000, a 2kW to 3kW on-grid system covers a significant portion of electricity needs. Homes with heavy summer air conditioning use may benefit from a 3kW or 5kW system.

faq.question: How much does rooftop solar cost in Prayagraj?
faq.answer: Pre-subsidy cost for a quality on-grid system in Prayagraj typically ranges from ₹60,000 to ₹80,000 per kW. After the PM Surya Ghar central subsidy (up to ₹78,000 for 3kW), the net cost is significantly lower. Get quotes from at least two PUVVNL-empanelled installers before deciding.

faq.question: Is Prayagraj a good location for rooftop solar?
faq.answer: Yes. Prayagraj receives approximately 5 to 5.5 peak sun hours per day on average — well suited for rooftop solar. Long, hot summers mean high solar generation coincides with peak electricity demand, making solar financially effective for most Prayagraj homeowners.

faq.question: Can businesses install rooftop solar?
faq.answer: Many commercial properties can install rooftop solar, subject to technical and regulatory requirements.

faq.question: Is solar suitable for small businesses?
faq.answer: Suitability depends on electricity consumption, roof availability and financial objectives.

faq.question: Can solar improve business profitability?
faq.answer: Reducing electricity expenses may improve long-term operating margins, depending on project performance and energy usage.

faq.question: How long do commercial solar systems last?
faq.answer: Commercial solar systems are typically designed for long operating lifetimes and often include extensive manufacturer warranties.
